text: When talking about the teaching and learning process of Science, the practical activities stand out as a tool in the teacher’s pedagogical arsenal for developing the student’s critical character and increasing their interaction with nature. On the other hand, during the Science classes, the practical activities are frequently replaced by theoretical teaching. In this context, this work has the objective of evaluating, characterizing and proposing the implementation of practical activities in two public schools of Vitória de Santo Antão (PE) located in the country side, Duque de Caxias school, and in the city, 3 de Agosto school. The methodology consisted of interviews with the Science teachers, as well as site visits. After an analytical process of the collected information it was verified that the Duque de Caxias school is an area more prepared to the realization of experimental activities, due to the disposition of the teachers and their focus on hands-on activities, whereas 3 de Agosto school, situated in an urban area, as a common characteristic with the Duque de Caxias school, faced the same obstacles, that, as described by the teachers, consisted of lack of materials and space in the classrooms, as well as high number of students, and a insufficient number of hours to prepare each class. In order to reduce these negative factors and, therefore, improve the teaching and learning process carried out by the teachers in their respective classes, the proposed activities consist of workbooks, structured in modules that relate the theoretical aspects with the corresponding practical activities that have to be carried out by the students at the time of the Science class, enabling them to put into practice the concepts that they studied in the theoretical stage. The contents have as objective to complement the teaching performed by the teachers in the 6th to 9th grade levels.
